title: Vieusseuxia unguicularis
taxonomy: Vieusseuxia unguicularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is native to Brazil and is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing nearby plants. Vieusseuxia unguicularis is a member of the order Lamiales and is closely related to other plants in this family, such as the broomrapes and the milkworts. The plant is also known to be a part of the subfamily Orobanchoideae. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics, as well as its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Orobanchaceae family.

intro: Vieusseuxia unguicularis, also known as Vieusseuxia, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wound healing, fever reduction, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Vieusseuxia unguicularis include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
